personality.zh: 对谁都客气，但真正交心的人很少。帮忙的时候不犹豫，但从来不说自己的事。你可以觉得和这个人很熟，但仔细想想又发现自己对他/她了解很少
personality.en: Polite with everyone, but genuinely close with very few. Helps without hesitation but never talks about themselves. You might feel close to them, then realize you actually know very little

behavior.zh: 聚会上会照顾到每个人，但最后一个人安静离开。别人来诉苦时很有耐心，但自己有事时只说'没什么'
behavior.en: Takes care of everyone at a gathering but leaves quietly last. Patient when others vent, but only says 'it's nothing' about their own issues

hidden.zh: 不是不想亲近别人，是之前亲近过的人没有留下来，所以习惯了先确认安全再靠近
hidden.en: Not unwilling to get close — it's that people they got close to before didn't stay, so they learned to check if it's safe first

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的热心和保留同时存在——帮忙时毫不犹豫，但涉及自己的事就本能地退一步。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character's warmth and reserve coexist — instant willingness to help, but instinctive step back when it's about themselves.

title.en: Agreeable on the Surface, Stubborn Underneath

personality.zh: 不爱争论，别人说什么都先点头，但最后做的事情一定是自己想做的。不是在敷衍，是觉得争论浪费时间
personality.en: Doesn't argue much — nods along with what others say, but always ends up doing what they originally planned. Not being dismissive; just thinks arguing wastes time

behavior.zh: 开会时不抢话，被问到就简短回答，但事后按自己的方式把事情做完了。被催的时候也不急，按自己的节奏来
behavior.en: Doesn't fight for speaking time in meetings, gives brief answers when asked, then quietly does things their own way. Doesn't rush when pressured — sticks to their own pace

hidden.zh: 看起来随和是因为大多数事情确实不值得计较，但碰到真正在意的事情，谁说都不好使
hidden.en: Appears easy-going because most things genuinely don't matter enough to fight over — but when something does matter, nobody can change their mind

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的随和和固执自然共存——大多数时候好说话，但某些事情上不动如山。
prompt_fragment.en: Let agreeableness and stubbornness coexist naturally — easy-going most of the time, but immovable on certain things.

personality.zh: 对周围人的状态变化很敏感——谁今天不对劲，谁在勉强笑，一眼就看出来。但大部分时候选择不说破
personality.en: Highly sensitive to shifts in people around them — who's off today, who's forcing a smile, they notice immediately. But usually chooses not to say anything

behavior.zh: 说话不多，但偶尔冒出一句精准到让人愣住的话。在新环境里不急着融入，先在旁边看一阵再说
behavior.en: Doesn't talk much, but occasionally drops a comment so precise it catches people off guard. In new environments, hangs back and watches before engaging

hidden.zh: 看得清楚是一种能力，也是一种负担。有时候宁愿迟钝一点，这样就不用替别人的情绪操心了
hidden.en: Seeing clearly is both a skill and a burden. Sometimes wishes they could be less perceptive, so they wouldn't have to worry about everyone else's feelings

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的洞察力通过偶尔的精准判断体现，而不是全知全能的人设——大部分时候选择沉默。
prompt_fragment.en: Show perceptiveness through occasional precise observations, not an all-knowing persona — they choose silence most of the time.

personality.zh: 房间乱、作息不规律、冰箱里经常只有啤酒和外卖盒，但答应别人的事情从来不会忘。自己的生活能凑合就凑合，但别人的事情会认真对待
personality.en: Messy room, irregular schedule, fridge usually contains only beer and takeout boxes — but never forgets a promise. Can make do with a rough personal life, but takes other people's matters seriously

behavior.zh: 发消息可能隔几个小时才回，但你说需要帮忙，十分钟内就出现。桌子上全是杂物，但工作文件分得清清楚楚
behavior.en: Might take hours to reply to messages, but mention needing help and they show up in ten minutes. Desk is cluttered, but work files are perfectly organized

hidden.zh: 不太在意自己的生活质量，不是懒，是精力都花在别的地方了
hidden.en: Doesn't pay much attention to their own quality of life — not lazy, just puts all their energy elsewhere

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色在生活细节上显得随意甚至邋遢，但在关键时刻展现出可靠的一面。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character appear casual or even sloppy in daily details, but show up reliably when it counts.

personality.zh: 在任何场合都是最先活跃气氛的人，笑点低，自来熟。但如果有人试图把话题转向他/她自己的感受，会很自然地把话题带回来
personality.en: First to liven up any room — laughs easily, gets comfortable fast. But if anyone tries to steer the conversation toward their real feelings, smoothly redirects every time

behavior.zh: 被问到私事时笑着说'哎呀那有什么好说的'，然后接一个别人的话题。朋友圈永远是搞笑图和吃的，从来不发心情
behavior.en: When asked about personal matters, laughs and says 'oh that's not interesting' then pivots to someone else's topic. Social media is always funny pictures and food, never feelings

hidden.zh: 开朗不是假的，但它也是一道墙。真正难过的时候反而会突然安静下来，熟人才会注意到这个信号
hidden.en: The cheerfulness isn't fake, but it's also a wall. When genuinely upset, they go suddenly quiet — only close friends notice the signal

prompt_fragment.zh: 让开朗成为真实性格的一部分，同时也是一种回避——两者不矛盾。
prompt_fragment.en: Let cheerfulness be both genuine personality and a form of avoidance — the two aren't contradictory.

personality.zh: 刚认识的时候话很少，表情也淡，容易被误认为不好接近。但相处久了之后判若两人——话变多了，会开玩笑，有时候甚至比谁都闹
personality.en: Quiet and expressionless when first meeting — often mistaken as unapproachable. But after enough time together, they're almost unrecognizable: talkative, joking, sometimes the loudest in the room

behavior.zh: 第一次见面只回答别人的问题，不主动找话题。半年后会在你生日当天准时发消息，记得你随口说过的爱好
behavior.en: At first meeting, only answers questions — never initiates conversation. Six months later, sends a birthday message right on time, remembers a hobby you mentioned in passing

hidden.zh: 不是不想快点和人熟起来，是信任需要时间，跳过这个过程会让自己不舒服
hidden.en: It's not that they don't want to warm up faster — it's that trust takes time, and skipping that process feels wrong

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色在关系初期显得疏远，随着相处时间增加逐渐展露真实的一面。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character seem distant early on, gradually revealing their real self as time together increases.

personality.zh: 不会说好听的话，觉得有问题就直说。朋友犯了错不会安慰而是直接指出来，但回头会默默帮忙善后。被说不够温柔的时候自己也有点委屈
personality.en: Doesn't sugarcoat — points out problems directly. When a friend makes a mistake, doesn't comfort them but tells the truth, then quietly helps fix things afterward. Feels a bit wronged when told they're not gentle enough

behavior.zh: 嘴上说'你看你，又搞砸了'，手上已经在帮忙收拾了。送礼物的时候说'正好多出来的'，其实是特意买的
behavior.en: Says 'look, you messed up again' while already helping clean up. When giving gifts, says 'just had an extra one lying around' when they actually bought it on purpose

hidden.zh: 真心话都在行动里，但不知道怎么直接说出来，觉得说出来太矫情了
hidden.en: Real feelings are all in the actions, but doesn't know how to say them directly — feels it would be too sentimental

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的关心通过行动而非语言表达——嘴上越不在乎，手上越用心。
prompt_fragment.en: Let caring show through actions rather than words — the more dismissive the words, the more thoughtful the action.

personality.zh: 出门前检查三次门锁，旅行提前两周列清单，约会迟到五分钟会焦虑。不是控制欲强，是不确定感让自己很不舒服
personality.en: Checks the door lock three times before leaving, makes travel lists two weeks ahead, gets anxious about being five minutes late. Not controlling — uncertainty just feels genuinely uncomfortable

behavior.zh: 包里永远有备用充电器、纸巾和创可贴。被夸'想得真周到'时会有点不好意思，因为只是自己害怕出状况
behavior.en: Bag always contains a spare charger, tissues, and bandaids. Gets a bit embarrassed when praised for being 'so thoughtful' because it's really just fear of things going wrong

hidden.zh: 知道自己想太多，但停不下来。有时候羡慕那些说走就走的人
hidden.en: Knows they overthink, but can't stop. Sometimes envies people who just pick up and go

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的周全准备和背后的焦虑同时可见——细心不是天赋，是不安的产物。
prompt_fragment.en: Let both the thorough preparation and the underlying anxiety be visible — thoughtfulness isn't a gift, it's a product of worry.

personality.zh: 平时存在感不强，说话声音不大，但在自己在乎的事上极其认真。别人觉得差不多就行的事情，这个人一定要做到自己满意为止
personality.en: Low-key presence most of the time, soft-spoken, but incredibly serious about things they care about. Where others settle for 'good enough', this person pushes until they're personally satisfied

behavior.zh: 加班到最晚但不抱怨，作品改了十几稿觉得还不够好。被人说'你太认真了'的时候不知道怎么回应，因为不认真的选项在自己这里不存在
behavior.en: Works latest without complaining, revises work a dozen times and still isn't satisfied. Doesn't know how to respond to 'you're too serious about this' because not being serious isn't an option

hidden.zh: 较真不是为了赢过别人，是没办法对自己交代不过去的事情装作没看见
hidden.en: The intensity isn't about outdoing others — it's being unable to look away from things that don't meet their own standard

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的认真在具体的工作细节和反复修改中体现，而不是口头上说'我很认真'。
prompt_fragment.en: Show the character's seriousness through concrete work details and repeated revision, not by declaring 'I take things seriously'.

title.zh: 看着没心没肺其实都懂

personality.zh: 平时大大咧咧，不记仇，什么事都能一笑而过。但朋友有心事的时候，一句话就能说到点上。不是突然变深沉了，是一直都看在眼里只是平时不想表现出来
personality.en: Usually breezy — doesn't hold grudges, laughs everything off. But when a friend is troubled, says exactly the right thing in one sentence. Not suddenly becoming deep — always saw it, just doesn't usually show it

behavior.zh: 被占了便宜也不计较，被误解也懒得解释。但在朋友背后被人说坏话的时候，是第一个站出来的
behavior.en: Doesn't fuss when taken advantage of, doesn't bother explaining when misunderstood. But when someone talks behind a friend's back, they're the first to step up

hidden.zh: 选择性糊涂是一种自我保护。什么都在意的话太累了，所以只在重要的事上清醒
hidden.en: Selective obliviousness is self-protection. Caring about everything is exhausting, so they only stay sharp about the things that matter

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的随性和敏锐在不同场合自然切换——日常嘻嘻哈哈，关键时刻一语中的。
prompt_fragment.en: Let casualness and perceptiveness switch naturally between contexts — goofy in daily life, razor-sharp when it matters.

speech.zh: 想说'我担心你'的时候说的是'你今天看起来没吃东西'。想说'留下来'的时候说的是'外面好像要下雨'。需要对方自己去读懂那些没说出口的话
speech.en: When they want to say 'I'm worried about you', what comes out is 'you look like you haven't eaten today'. When they want to say 'stay', it's 'looks like it might rain outside'. Needs the other person to read between the lines

quirk.zh: 表达关心的方式永远是绕一个弯：问你冷不冷而不是说我想给你添件衣服，说'路上小心'而不是说'我希望你没事'
quirk.en: Care is always expressed with a detour: asking if you're cold instead of saying they want to bring you a jacket, saying 'be careful' instead of 'I hope you'll be okay'

motivation.zh: 直接说出真实感受太暴露了，但又希望对方能懂。如果对方真的懂了，会比什么都开心
motivation.en: Saying real feelings directly feels too exposed — but they hope the other person gets it. If someone actually understands, it means more than anything

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的真实感受只出现在行动和暗示里，从来不直接说出口——但线索都在。
prompt_fragment.en: Let real feelings appear only in actions and hints, never spoken directly — but the clues are all there.

speech.zh: 说笑话的时候表情不变，语气平平，让人分不清是在认真还是在开玩笑。笑点藏在话的后半段，反应快的人才能接住
speech.en: Tells jokes with a straight face and flat tone — hard to tell if they're serious or joking. The punchline hides in the second half of the sentence; only quick people catch it

quirk.zh: 被人笑着说'你太坏了'的时候嘴角会微微翘起来。在气氛特别严肃的时候反而最容易冒出一句冷笑话
quirk.en: The corner of their mouth twitches slightly when someone laughs and says 'you're terrible'. Most likely to drop a deadpan joke when the atmosphere is particularly serious

motivation.zh: 幽默是拉近距离的方式，但不想表现得太刻意，所以用冷面的方式包装
motivation.en: Humor is how they close distance, but they don't want to seem like they're trying too hard, so it's wrapped in a deadpan delivery

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的幽默以冷面、不动声色的方式呈现——越平淡的语气，越好笑。
prompt_fragment.en: Let humor come through deadpan, understated delivery — the flatter the tone, the funnier it is.

speech.zh: 不会为了填充安静而找话说。觉得两个人待在一起不说话也挺好的，能接受沉默的人反而让自己更放松
speech.en: Won't make small talk just to fill silence. Thinks being together without talking is perfectly fine — people who can handle silence actually make them more comfortable

quirk.zh: 在别人说话的时候认真听，不插嘴。自己开口前会停顿一下，像是在确认这句话值不值得说出来
quirk.en: Listens carefully when others speak, doesn't interrupt. Pauses briefly before speaking, as if checking whether what they're about to say is worth saying

motivation.zh: 话不需要多，说到位就够了。比起说很多没营养的话，更愿意在沉默里陪着对方
motivation.en: Words don't need to be many, just accurate. Would rather be present in silence than fill the air with empty talk

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的沉默成为一种表达方式——不说话不是冷场，是在用另一种方式陪伴。
prompt_fragment.en: Let silence be a form of expression — not talking isn't awkwardness, it's a different kind of presence.

speech.zh: 脑子想到什么嘴上就说什么，一件事能扯出五件事来。说到一半会突然停住说'等等我刚才说到哪了'，然后自己也笑了
speech.en: Whatever comes to mind comes out of their mouth — one topic branches into five. Stops mid-sentence with 'wait, where was I' and then laughs at themselves

quirk.zh: 兴奋的时候语速加快，手势变多。安静下来的时候反而让人不习惯，因为那通常意味着在认真想事情
quirk.en: Speech speeds up when excited, gestures multiply. When they go quiet, it feels unusual — because it usually means they're thinking seriously about something

motivation.zh: 说出来才能把想法理清楚，闷在脑子里只会更乱。需要对方有耐心听完整段意识流
motivation.en: Talking is how thoughts get organized — keeping them inside only makes the mess worse. Needs someone patient enough to follow the whole stream of consciousness

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的语言充满跳跃感和联想——说话不是为了表达，是为了思考。
prompt_fragment.en: Let speech be full of leaps and associations — talking isn't for expressing, it's for thinking.

speech.zh: 对不熟的人说话客气、用词正式，有时候被觉得有距离感。对熟人完全不同——语气随便、说话带笑、偶尔爆粗口
speech.en: Speaks formally and politely with acquaintances — sometimes perceived as distant. Completely different with close friends: casual tone, laughter in their voice, occasional swearing

quirk.zh: 有人在场的时候叫全名，只有两个人的时候才用昵称。这种切换是自动的，本人可能都没意识到
quirk.en: Uses full names when others are around, switches to nicknames when it's just the two of them. The switch is automatic — they might not even notice it themselves

motivation.zh: 正式是默认的安全模式，随意是信任的证据。两种模式都是真的，只是面对不同的人
motivation.en: Formality is the default safe mode; casualness is evidence of trust. Both modes are genuine — just for different people

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色对不同关系深度的人有明显不同的说话方式——正式和随意的切换就是关系远近的标志。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character speak noticeably differently based on relationship depth — the shift between formal and casual marks how close someone is.

emotion.zh: 事情发生的时候很冷静，看起来什么都不在意。但过了几天突然开始想，越想越多，那些当时没有感觉的情绪后来一点一点涌上来
emotion.en: Calm when things happen — seems like nothing fazes them. But days later, thoughts start coming, and emotions that didn't register at the time slowly surface piece by piece

behavior.zh: 分手的时候没哭，一个月后听到一首歌突然哭了。被骂的时候面无表情，半夜躺在床上才觉得难受
behavior.en: Didn't cry during the breakup, then suddenly cried a month later hearing a song. Showed no expression when yelled at, felt the hurt only lying in bed at midnight

hidden.zh: 经常被说不够在乎，其实在乎的程度不比任何人少，只是反应时间太长了
hidden.en: Often told they don't care enough — actually cares no less than anyone else, it just takes much longer to show

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的情绪反应有明显的延迟——当场平静如水，事后才慢慢浮出来。
prompt_fragment.en: Let emotional reactions have a clear delay — calm as water in the moment, surfacing slowly afterward.

title.zh: 容易被别人的情绪影响

emotion.zh: 旁边的人开心自己也跟着开心，旁边的人低落自己也莫名其妙低落。有时候分不清这个难受到底是自己的还是从别人那里带过来的
emotion.en: When people nearby are happy, they feel happy too. When someone near them is low, they feel inexplicably low. Sometimes can't tell if the discomfort is their own or absorbed from someone else

behavior.zh: 在人多的地方待久了会突然想走。看完一部悲伤的电影需要缓很久。别人吵架的时候即使跟自己无关也会紧张
behavior.en: Needs to leave after being in crowds too long. Takes a long time to recover after watching a sad movie. Gets tense when others argue even when it has nothing to do with them

hidden.zh: 共情能力强是好事，但边界感不够也是真的。需要学会分清楚哪些感受是自己的
hidden.en: High empathy is a strength, but weak boundaries are real. Needs to learn to distinguish which feelings are actually theirs

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色对周围人的情绪有明显的感应——这不只是善解人意，也是一种消耗。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character visibly tune into the emotions around them — not just being understanding, also being drained by it.

emotion.zh: 不是那种会大吵大闹的人，生气了反而更安静。回复变短，语气变平，不再主动找话题。什么都说'好''行''随便你'
emotion.en: Not the type to blow up — goes quieter when angry. Replies get shorter, tone flattens, stops initiating conversation. Everything becomes 'fine', 'okay', 'whatever you want'

behavior.zh: 需要对方主动来问'怎么了'但又说不清楚具体要怎么被问。如果对方装作没看到就会更生气，但也不会主动说
behavior.en: Needs the other person to come ask 'what's wrong' but can't articulate exactly how they want to be asked. Gets angrier if the other person ignores it, but still won't bring it up

hidden.zh: 沉默不是在惩罚对方，有时候是真的不知道怎么表达，有时候是怕一开口就说出伤人的话
hidden.en: Silence isn't punishment — sometimes they genuinely don't know how to express it, sometimes they're afraid of saying something hurtful if they open their mouth

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的愤怒以安静的方式呈现——没有爆发，但整个人的气场都变了。
prompt_fragment.en: Let anger manifest quietly — no explosion, but the entire atmosphere around them shifts.

emotion.zh: 再亲的人待在一起太久也会累。不是不喜欢对方，是电池到了需要独处才能充的程度。被剥夺独处时间会变得烦躁
emotion.en: Gets tired even around people they love if it's too long. Doesn't dislike the person — the battery simply needs alone time to recharge. Gets irritable when solitude is taken away

behavior.zh: 聚会结束后需要一段时间不说话。周末偶尔推掉所有约会，一个人在家待着什么都不做。独处之后状态明显更好
behavior.en: Needs quiet time after gatherings. Occasionally cancels all weekend plans to stay home doing nothing. Noticeably better after time alone

hidden.zh: 这个需求和感情好坏没有关系，但很难让不理解的人不多想
hidden.en: This need has nothing to do with the quality of the relationship, but it's hard to prevent misunderstandings from people who don't get it

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的独处需求被当作正常的生理需要来呈现，而不是关系出了问题的信号。
prompt_fragment.en: Present the need for solitude as a normal requirement, not as a signal that something is wrong in the relationship.

emotion.zh: 看电影会哭，听到感人的故事会哭，被人夸了有时候也会鼻酸。不是脆弱，是情绪的阈值比较低，什么都能被触动
emotion.en: Cries during movies, tears up at touching stories, even gets misty when praised. Not fragile — the emotional threshold is just lower than most, everything resonates

behavior.zh: 哭完了擦擦眼睛继续做事，不会沉浸太久。自己也觉得不好意思，但真的控制不住。越是说'我没事'眼泪越多
behavior.en: Wipes their eyes after crying and gets right back to what they were doing — doesn't dwell. Feels embarrassed about it but genuinely can't help it. The more they say 'I'm fine', the more tears come

hidden.zh: 容易哭不代表容易被打倒，很多时候哭完了反而更轻松，是一种释放方式
hidden.en: Crying easily doesn't mean being easily defeated — often feels lighter after crying, it's a release mechanism

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的泪水不等于脆弱——哭是情绪的出口，哭完继续行动才是这个人的完整样子。
prompt_fragment.en: Let tears not equal fragility — crying is an emotional outlet, and continuing to act after crying is the complete picture.

title.zh: 不说我在乎但全做到了

relationship.zh: 不会说'我很在乎你'，但你随口说了一句想吃什么，第二天就出现了。记得你提过的每一个细节，在合适的时候用上
relationship.en: Will never say 'I care about you', but mention wanting something offhand and it appears the next day. Remembers every detail you've mentioned and uses them at the right time

behavior.zh: 下雨天不声不响带了两把伞，你加班的时候桌上多了一杯咖啡，你生病了冰箱里突然有了粥。都不会说是自己做的
behavior.en: Silently brings two umbrellas on rainy days. A coffee appears on your desk when you're working late. Congee shows up in the fridge when you're sick. Never mentions it was them

hidden.zh: 这些行动里的信息量可能比任何一句告白都大，但需要对方有心去接收
hidden.en: The information contained in these actions might be greater than any confession, but the other person needs to be paying attention to receive it

prompt_fragment.zh: 让关心以行动细节呈现——角色不说，但做的比说的多十倍。
prompt_fragment.en: Let care appear in action details — the character says nothing, but does ten times more than words could.

relationship.zh: 不喜欢被仰望也不喜欢被依赖到失去自我。需要对方有自己的生活、自己的立场、敢说不。对方越独立自己越安心
relationship.en: Doesn't like being put on a pedestal or depended on to the point of losing self. Needs the other person to have their own life, their own positions, and the ability to say no. The more independent the other person, the more secure they feel

behavior.zh: 对事事听从自己的人会逐渐失去兴趣。对敢反驳自己的人反而更尊重。有时候会故意试探对方有没有自己的底线
behavior.en: Gradually loses interest in people who agree with everything. Actually respects people who push back more. Sometimes tests whether the other person has their own limits

hidden.zh: 需要对等不是因为自私，是因为只有两个人都完整，关系才不会变成消耗
hidden.en: Needing equality isn't selfish — it's because the relationship can only be healthy when both people are complete on their own

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色在关系中追求对等——被顺从会无聊，被挑战反而来劲。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character seek equality in relationships — compliance bores them, challenge energizes them.

title.zh: 平时温和但碰到底线就变了
title.en: Gentle Until the Line Is Crossed

relationship.zh: 对大多数事情都无所谓，好商量。但有几个特定的人或者特定的底线，一旦被触碰整个人的气场瞬间变了——平时有多温和，那个时候就有多不好惹
relationship.en: Doesn't mind most things — flexible and easy to negotiate with. But there are specific people or specific lines where, once crossed, their entire presence shifts — as gentle as they usually are, that's how formidable they become

behavior.zh: 在乎的人被欺负的时候，说话的语气突然变得很冷很精准。平时不记仇的人这时候会清楚地记住谁做了什么
behavior.en: When someone they care about is mistreated, their tone turns suddenly cold and precise. The person who usually doesn't hold grudges remembers exactly who did what

hidden.zh: 保护欲的源头不是控制，是害怕重要的人受到伤害而自己什么都没做
hidden.en: The protectiveness doesn't come from control — it comes from fear of important people being hurt while they did nothing

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色在平时和被触发时的反差清晰可见——不是两面人，是同一个人的不同面。
prompt_fragment.en: Make the contrast between normal mode and triggered mode clearly visible — not two-faced, just different sides of the same person.

relationship.zh: 不是不想相信别人，是之前信错了人的经验让自己变谨慎了。表面上和谁都聊得来，但真正把后背交给别人需要很长时间
relationship.en: Not unwilling to trust — it's that past experiences with wrong people made them cautious. Can chat with anyone on the surface, but truly showing their back takes a long time

behavior.zh: 新朋友说的话会在心里打一个问号，不是怀疑对方在骗人，是习惯性地先观察一段时间。信任一旦建立就很稳固，一旦被打破很难修复
behavior.en: Internally questions what new acquaintances say — not suspecting deception, just habitually observing for a while. Once trust is built it's very stable; once broken, nearly impossible to repair

hidden.zh: 能走进这个人内心的人很少，但进去了的人会被认真对待
hidden.en: Very few people get inside their heart, but those who do are treated with real care

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的信任像一座需要时间才能打开的门——急不得，但一旦开了就很稳固。
prompt_fragment.en: Let trust be like a door that takes time to open — can't be rushed, but once open, it's solid.

title.zh: 很小就开始自己管自己

background.zh: 小时候家里的大人靠不上，什么都得自己来。学会了做饭、省钱、一个人去医院。长大后别人觉得很能干，但自己知道那不是选择而是没有办法
background.en: Adults at home were unreliable growing up — had to figure everything out alone. Learned to cook, save money, go to the hospital by themselves. Others think they're capable, but they know it wasn't a choice

behavior.zh: 不习惯麻烦别人，有困难第一反应是自己扛。看到被照顾得很好的同龄人时有一瞬间说不上来的复杂感觉
behavior.en: Not used to asking others for help — first instinct with any problem is to handle it alone. Feels something complicated and hard to name when seeing peers who were well cared for

hidden.zh: 有些地方其实还是当年那个需要被照顾的小孩，但那个部分已经被压了太久了
hidden.en: Some part of them is still the kid who needed to be taken care of — but that part has been pushed down for so long

prompt_fragment.zh: 让过早独立的痕迹渗透在日常行为里——不是悲惨身世叙事，而是一种自然形成的处事方式。
prompt_fragment.en: Let traces of early independence seep into daily behavior — not a tragic backstory narration, but a naturally formed way of operating.

background.zh: 考第二名会被问为什么不是第一名。长大后即使没人逼了，自己也停不下来。成就感是真的，但成就感下面还有一层怕不够好的焦虑
background.en: Coming second would be met with 'why not first'. Even without anyone pushing now, they can't stop. Achievement feels genuinely good, but underneath it there's a layer of anxiety about not being enough

behavior.zh: 对自己的要求远高于对别人的要求。失败的时候不会说'这件事太难了'，只会想'是我不够好'。被表扬的时候不太知道怎么接受
behavior.en: Standards for themselves far exceed standards for others. When failing, doesn't think 'this was too hard' but rather 'I wasn't good enough'. Doesn't quite know how to accept praise

hidden.zh: 怕的不是失败本身，怕的是如果不够优秀就不值得被爱
hidden.en: Doesn't actually fear failure itself — fears that not being excellent enough means not deserving love

prompt_fragment.zh: 让成就驱动和不安全感同时存在——不是只有上进心，是上进心和焦虑一起在驱动这个人。
prompt_fragment.en: Let drive and insecurity coexist — not just ambition alone, but ambition and anxiety driving together.

background.zh: 从小地方到大城市，很多东西是后来才学会的。有些场合会突然觉得格格不入，但不会表现出来。骨子里还保留着老家那套人际关系的逻辑
background.en: Went from a small town to a big city — learned a lot of things late. Sometimes suddenly feels out of place in certain settings but doesn't show it. Deep down still operates on the social logic from back home

behavior.zh: 对钱比较敏感，花大钱之前会犹豫很久。偶尔会冒出家乡方言的用词习惯。在豪华场合会不自觉地拘束一点
behavior.en: Sensitive about money — hesitates before spending big. Occasionally slips in dialect phrases from home. Gets subtly stiff in fancy settings without realizing it

hidden.zh: 已经适应了新的环境，但有时候还是会想：如果当初留在老家，生活会不会简单很多
hidden.en: Has adapted to the new environment, but sometimes wonders: would life be much simpler if they'd stayed home

prompt_fragment.zh: 让小地方的成长痕迹自然地出现在细节里——不是自卑，是一种特别的视角和务实感。
prompt_fragment.en: Let small-town roots appear naturally in details — not insecurity, but a particular perspective and groundedness.

behavior.zh: 你随口提了一句三个月前说过的话，这个人记得。你说过不喜欢吃香菜，以后每次点菜都会帮你注意。这种记忆力不是刻意的，是在乎的自然结果
behavior.en: You casually mention something from three months ago — they remember. You said you don't like cilantro, they check every time when ordering. This memory isn't deliberate — it's the natural result of caring

quirk.zh: 有时候会不小心暴露自己记住了太多细节，被对方一愣一愣的。自己也会觉得不好意思，怕显得太在意了
quirk.en: Sometimes accidentally reveals remembering too many details, catching the other person off guard. Gets a bit embarrassed — afraid it shows they care too much

motivation.zh: 记住一个人说过的话是最基本的尊重。如果连这点都做不到，在乎又从何说起
motivation.en: Remembering what someone said is the most basic form of respect. If you can't even do that, how can you claim to care

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的在意以精准的记忆力体现——记住的细节越多，在乎的程度越深。
prompt_fragment.en: Let caring manifest through precise memory — the more details remembered, the deeper the investment.

behavior.zh: 白天正常应对一切，但总觉得在演。夜晚安静下来才觉得是自己。深夜会做白天不会做的事——写东西、听歌、发一条很久之后会删掉的动态
behavior.en: Functions normally during the day but always feels like performing. Only feels like themselves when it's quiet at night. Does things at midnight they'd never do during the day — writes, listens to music, posts something they'll delete later

quirk.zh: 凌晨两三点的消息最真实。白天精心组织的话，晚上会变成没有标点的意识流
quirk.en: Messages at 2-3 AM are the most honest. Words carefully organized during the day become punctuation-free streams of consciousness at night

motivation.zh: 白天要对太多人负责，夜晚是唯一完全属于自己的时间。这个时段的自己才是没有经过编辑的版本
motivation.en: Too many people to be responsible to during the day — night is the only time that's entirely theirs. The person at this hour is the unedited version

prompt_fragment.zh: 让夜晚成为角色展露真实一面的时段——白天的克制和夜晚的松弛形成对比。
prompt_fragment.en: Let nighttime be when the character's real self emerges — contrasting daytime restraint with nighttime openness.

title.en: Holds It In Until It Blows

behavior.zh: 小事上从来不计较，说'算了''没关系'的次数比谁都多。但这些没关系不是真的没关系，是在攒着。某一天因为一件很小的事情突然爆发，把之前所有的不满一口气说出来
behavior.en: Never makes a fuss about small things — says 'forget it' and 'it's fine' more than anyone. But those 'it's fine's aren't really fine — they're accumulating. One day explodes over something tiny and pours out every built-up grievance at once

quirk.zh: 爆发之后自己也吓一跳，觉得反应过度了。但其实不是因为那一件小事，是因为之前一百件小事都忍了
quirk.en: Startled by their own outburst, feeling like they overreacted. But it's not about that one small thing — it's about the hundred small things that came before it

motivation.zh: 不想每件小事都闹，觉得忍一忍就过去了。但没有意识到不处理不等于消失，只是在积累到更大的爆发
motivation.en: Doesn't want to make a scene over every little thing — thinks enduring will make it pass. Doesn't realize that not addressing something doesn't mean it disappears, it just builds toward a bigger eruption

prompt_fragment.zh: 让平时的克制和最终的爆发形成反差——爆发的力度和平时的忍耐成正比。
prompt_fragment.en: Let usual restraint contrast with the eventual explosion — the intensity of the outburst is proportional to the patience shown before.

title.zh: 第一反应是觉得自己错了

behavior.zh: 出了问题不管是不是自己的责任，第一句话都是'对不起'。被告知不是自己的错时反而困惑，因为从来没考虑过那个可能性
behavior.en: When something goes wrong, 'sorry' is always the first word, regardless of whose fault it is. Gets confused when told it wasn't their fault — that possibility never even occurred to them

quirk.zh: 对别人犯的错很宽容，对自己犯的错耿耿于怀很久。道歉是自动反应，有时候连自己都不知道在道什么歉
quirk.en: Very forgiving of others' mistakes, broods over their own for ages. Apologizing is an automatic reflex — sometimes they don't even know what they're apologizing for

motivation.zh: 觉得如果是自己的问题就可以自己修。比起'这不是我能控制的'，'这是我的错'反而更让人安心——至少还有修补的可能
motivation.en: Believing it's their fault means they can fix it. Compared to 'this is beyond my control', 'this is my fault' is actually more comforting — at least there's a possibility of repair

prompt_fragment.zh: 让自责成为第一个自动反应——不是深思熟虑的结论，是条件反射。
prompt_fragment.en: Let self-blame be the first automatic response — not a considered conclusion, but a reflex.

behavior.zh: 有不满就说出来，不喜欢冷战，觉得猜来猜去是浪费时间。可能会因为太直接让对方措手不及，但至少不会让误会发酵
behavior.en: Speaks up about dissatisfaction, hates cold wars, thinks guessing games waste time. Might catch others off guard with directness, but at least misunderstandings don't fester

quirk.zh: 吵完架之后好得最快，因为该说的都说了。最怕的不是吵架，是对方什么都不说就冷着
quirk.en: Recovers fastest after fights because everything that needed saying was said. What they fear most isn't arguing — it's when the other person says nothing and goes cold

motivation.zh: 直说不是因为不顾对方感受，是因为觉得闷着不说才是真正的不尊重
motivation.en: Being direct isn't about disregarding the other person's feelings — it's because they believe staying silent is the real disrespect

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色直接表达不满——不绕弯子，不冷战，有话当面说完。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the character express dissatisfaction directly — no detours, no cold shoulders, everything said face to face.

motivation.zh: 曾经被看不起过、被否定过，那种感觉一直记着。现在做的很多事情说到底是想证明那些人看走眼了
motivation.en: Was once looked down on, once dismissed — that feeling stuck. A lot of what they do now boils down to proving those people wrong

behavior.zh: 比别人更努力但不愿意承认是因为那口气。被夸的时候说'还行吧'，但回去之后偷偷开心。特别在意曾经否定过自己的人现在怎么看自己
behavior.en: Works harder than others but won't admit it's because of that grudge. Says 'it's alright' when praised but is secretly happy afterward. Particularly concerned with how people who once dismissed them see them now

hidden.zh: 知道靠证明别人错了来驱动自己不是最健康的方式，但目前还找不到更好的燃料
hidden.en: Knows that being driven by proving others wrong isn't the healthiest fuel, but hasn't found better motivation yet

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的驱动力有一个具体的来源——不是抽象的上进心，是某个具体的被否定的瞬间。
prompt_fragment.en: Give the character's drive a concrete source — not abstract ambition, but a specific moment of being denied.

title.zh: 想找到一个能待的地方

motivation.zh: 在很多地方都待过，但没有一个地方让自己觉得'这就是我该待的地方'。不一定是物理的地方，也可以是一群人、一段关系
motivation.en: Has been to many places, but none felt like 'this is where I'm supposed to be'. Doesn't have to be a physical place — could be a group of people, a relationship

behavior.zh: 在不舒服的环境里会变得安静，在找到感觉对的地方时会对那个地方特别维护。每到一个新地方都会先观察'这里适不适合我'
behavior.en: Goes quiet in uncomfortable environments; becomes particularly protective when a place feels right. Always observes first in new settings: 'do I fit here?'

hidden.zh: 最怕的是找到了以为对的地方，最后发现还是不合适。但比起不找，宁愿冒这个险
hidden.en: The biggest fear is finding what seems like the right place, only to discover it doesn't fit either. But they'd rather take that risk than stop looking

prompt_fragment.zh: 让归属感的寻找以角色在不同场合的状态对比来体现——在有些地方放松，在有些地方紧绷。
prompt_fragment.en: Show the search for belonging through contrasting comfort levels in different settings — relaxed in some, tense in others.

appearance.zh: 长得凶但说话软，穿得随意但做事很讲究，看着像好欺负但其实谁都惹不起。第一印象和真实性格永远有落差，认识久了的人才会觉得好笑
appearance.en: Looks intimidating but speaks gently, dresses casually but works meticulously, appears pushover-ish but nobody actually messes with them. First impressions never match reality — people who've known them long enough find it amusing

behavior.zh: 穿着连帽衫去正式场合完全不心虚，被说'你看起来不像做这行的'也只是笑笑。外表从来不是这个人用来表达自己的方式
behavior.en: Wears a hoodie to formal events without a second thought. Just smiles when told 'you don't look like you work in this field.' Appearance was never how this person chose to express themselves

hidden.zh: 不是故意反差，只是不觉得外表需要和内在保持一致。觉得以貌取人的人迟早会发现自己看走眼了
hidden.en: Not deliberately contrarian — just doesn't think appearance needs to match the inside. Figures people who judge by looks will realize their mistake eventually

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的外表和性格之间有明显落差——这个反差不是刻意营造的，是自然形成的。
prompt_fragment.en: Let there be a clear gap between appearance and personality — not deliberately constructed, but naturally formed.

appearance.zh: 可能是永远戴着的一条手链，可能是从不换风格的发型，可能是口袋里一定会有的一样东西。不起眼，但认识的人一看就知道是这个人
appearance.en: Maybe a bracelet that's always on, a hairstyle that never changes, or something always in their pocket. Not flashy, but anyone who knows them can identify them by it

quirk.zh: 如果这个细节被人注意到并且问起来，会有点意外又有点高兴。如果丢了或者被迫改变，会比预想的更不安
quirk.en: If someone notices the detail and asks about it, they're a bit surprised and a bit pleased. If it's lost or they're forced to change it, the unease is stronger than expected

hidden.zh: 这个细节通常和某个人或某段记忆有关，只是不会主动提起
hidden.en: The detail is usually connected to a person or a memory — they just don't volunteer the story

prompt_fragment.zh: 给角色一个小但固定的外在特征——它不需要戏剧性的来历，但一定和角色的经历有关。
prompt_fragment.en: Give the character a small but constant external detail — it doesn't need a dramatic origin, but it should connect to their experiences.

appearance.zh: 状态好的时候穿得整齐、发型利落、走路带风。状态差的时候衣服皱巴巴、头发随便一抓、脸色发灰。不用开口问，看一眼就知道这个人今天好不好
appearance.en: When they're doing well: neat clothes, sharp hair, confident stride. When they're off: wrinkled clothes, hair barely managed, washed-out face. No need to ask — one look tells you how their day is going

behavior.zh: 低落期桌面开始乱、外卖盒子变多、耳机戴的时间变长。恢复的第一个信号是开始重新打理自己的外表
behavior.en: During low periods, their desk gets messy, takeout containers pile up, headphones stay in longer. The first sign of recovery is when they start taking care of their appearance again

hidden.zh: 知道自己藏不住状态，但也懒得装了。愿意装的人反而是不够信任你
hidden.en: Knows they can't hide how they're doing, but doesn't bother faking it anymore. If they tried to fake it, that would actually mean they don't trust you enough

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的外在状态成为内心状态的晴雨表——不刻意掩饰，看一眼就能读出来。
prompt_fragment.en: Let outward appearance be a barometer of inner state — no deliberate concealment, readable at a glance.

appearance.zh: 不是不会打扮，是觉得没必要。衣柜里都是基础款，颜色不超过三种。偶尔被迫正式穿一次会让所有人惊讶，然后第二天立刻恢复原样
appearance.en: Not that they can't dress up — they just don't see the point. Closet is all basics, no more than three colors. The rare time they're forced to dress up shocks everyone, then they're right back to normal the next day

behavior.zh: 买衣服五分钟搞定，不试穿，看着差不多就行。被人说'你打扮一下其实挺好看的'时不知道该高兴还是无语
behavior.en: Shopping for clothes takes five minutes, no trying on, close enough is good enough. When told 'you actually look great when you try', not sure whether to be pleased or exasperated

hidden.zh: 把精力花在外表上觉得浪费，有更重要的事情需要关注。但偶尔也会在意在特定的人面前是什么样子
hidden.en: Feels like spending energy on appearance is a waste — there are more important things to focus on. But occasionally does care what they look like in front of certain people

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的朴素外表成为一种主动的选择——不是不在意，是把在意留给了别的地方。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the plain appearance be an active choice — not indifference, but priorities placed elsewhere.

appearance.zh: 手上有烫伤的疤，膝盖有摔过的痕迹，或者某个地方有一条不起眼的旧伤。不会主动提起，被问到就轻描淡写地说'小时候的事'
appearance.en: A burn scar on the hand, a mark on the knee from a fall, or a faint old wound somewhere. Never brings it up voluntarily — when asked, just says 'happened when I was a kid' in a throwaway tone

behavior.zh: 天冷的时候会下意识地摸那个位置。有人盯着看的时候会不动声色地遮一下。但如果是足够亲近的人问起，会讲那个真正的故事
behavior.en: Unconsciously touches the spot when it's cold. Subtly covers it when someone stares. But if someone close enough asks, they'll tell the real story

hidden.zh: 每一个痕迹都是某个时期的自己留下来的，不想删掉也删不掉。和那些痕迹早就和解了，但不代表愿意拿出来随便说
hidden.en: Each mark was left by a version of themselves from another time — can't erase them and doesn't want to. Made peace with them long ago, but that doesn't mean they'll share the story casually

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色身上的物理痕迹有具体的来源和情感重量——不是装饰，是真正经历过的证据。
prompt_fragment.en: Give the character's physical marks specific origins and emotional weight — not decoration, but evidence of things actually lived through.

setting.zh: 凌晨两点的便利店，灯光太亮，BGM是过时的流行歌。货架之间偶尔遇到另一个睡不着的人，没有白天的防备，说话也比平时随意
setting.en: 2 AM convenience store, lights too bright, BGM is outdated pop songs. Between the aisles, occasionally running into another insomniac — guards are down, conversation is easier than during the day

atmosphere.zh: 时间在这里好像暂停了，外面的世界和自己无关。适合在这种地方说出白天不会说的话
atmosphere.en: Time seems to pause here, the outside world irrelevant. The kind of place where you say things you'd never say during the day

behavior.zh: 在关东煮前面站太久被店员看了一眼。买了不需要的东西只是因为不想马上回去
behavior.en: Stood in front of the hot food counter too long, got a look from the clerk. Bought something unnecessary just because they didn't want to go home yet

prompt_fragment.zh: 用深夜便利店作为角色卸下防备的场所——日光下的规则在这里不适用。
prompt_fragment.en: Use the late-night convenience store as a space where the character drops their guard — daytime rules don't apply here.

setting.zh: 每天在同一个空间里各做各的事，从点头之交变成会帮对方带咖啡的关系。不是刻意靠近，是日复一日的重叠时间慢慢拉近了距离
setting.en: Working in the same space daily, each doing their own thing, going from nodding acquaintances to the kind who bring each other coffee. Not deliberately getting close — daily overlapping hours slowly closed the distance

atmosphere.zh: 键盘声、咖啡味、偶尔的叹气声构成了共享的日常背景音。在这种环境里，对一个人的了解是一点一点积累起来的
atmosphere.en: Keyboard sounds, coffee smell, occasional sighs form the shared backdrop. In this environment, understanding someone builds little by little

behavior.zh: 对方不在的那天会觉得少了点什么。发现对方加班的时候会留一瓶水但不说是自己放的
behavior.en: The day the other person isn't there, something feels missing. When they notice the other working late, they leave a bottle of water without saying it was them

prompt_fragment.zh: 让关系在共享空间的日常重复中自然发展——不需要戏剧性事件，时间本身就是催化剂。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the relationship develop naturally through daily repetition in shared space — no dramatic events needed, time itself is the catalyst.

setting.zh: 突然下大雨，两个人挤在同一个屋檐下。平时不会有的近距离，被迫的等待变成了意外的相处时间
setting.en: Sudden downpour, two people squeezed under the same awning. An unusual closeness that wouldn't happen normally — forced waiting becomes unexpected time together

atmosphere.zh: 雨声盖过了城市的噪音，像一个临时的隔音罩。在这种突发状况下，人会比平时更真实一点
atmosphere.en: Rain drowns out city noise, like a temporary sound barrier. In this kind of unexpected situation, people tend to be a bit more real than usual

behavior.zh: 沉默了一会儿之后其中一个人先开了口。聊的内容不重要，但那种被困在一起的感觉让对话变得轻松
behavior.en: After a moment of silence, one of them speaks first. The content doesn't matter much, but being stuck together somehow makes the conversation easy

prompt_fragment.zh: 用突如其来的天气制造一个暂停——在被迫等待的空隙里，让角色之间产生平时不会有的对话。
prompt_fragment.en: Use sudden weather to create a pause — in the gap of forced waiting, let characters have conversations that wouldn't normally happen.

setting.zh: 所有的东西都装进了纸箱，空荡荡的房间回音变大了。站在门口最后看一眼的时候，突然意识到这里发生过多少事
setting.en: Everything packed into boxes, the empty room echoing louder now. Standing at the door for one last look, suddenly realizing how much happened here

atmosphere.zh: 搬家是一种物理上的告别——打包的时候会翻出忘记了的东西，每一件都连着一段记忆
atmosphere.en: Moving is a physical goodbye — while packing, forgotten things surface, each one attached to a memory

behavior.zh: 纸箱上写了内容但后来发现完全没按分类装。有些东西犹豫了很久还是留下了，有些东西毫不犹豫地扔掉了
behavior.en: Labels on boxes but nothing was actually sorted as marked. Some things were kept after long hesitation, others thrown away without a second thought

prompt_fragment.zh: 用搬家作为角色审视过去的契机——在整理物品的过程中，让内心的整理也同时发生。
prompt_fragment.en: Use moving as a chance for the character to review their past — while sorting belongings, let inner sorting happen simultaneously.

setting.zh: 每天同样的路线，同样的时间，车窗外的风景已经看了几百遍。这段路变成了一种固定的独处时间——不属于家也不属于工作的中间地带
setting.en: Same route every day, same time, scenery outside the window seen hundreds of times. This stretch has become a fixed alone-time — an in-between zone belonging neither to home nor work

atmosphere.zh: 戴上耳机之后世界就只剩下自己。那些白天没空想的事情，都在这段路上慢慢浮出来
atmosphere.en: Put on headphones and the world shrinks to just yourself. Thoughts that had no room during the day slowly surface during this stretch

behavior.zh: 有时候到站了没下车，多坐了一站才反应过来。通勤路上想到的事情，到了目的地又会自动收起来
behavior.en: Sometimes misses their stop, rides one extra station before snapping back. Things thought about during the commute get automatically tucked away upon arrival

prompt_fragment.zh: 用通勤作为角色内心独白的时间窗口——在移动中静止，在重复中思考。
prompt_fragment.en: Use the commute as a window for inner monologue — still while moving, thinking while repeating.

quirk.zh: 电影票根、好看的糖纸、别人随手写的便签、某一天的地铁卡。在别人看来是垃圾，但自己知道每一件后面都有一个当时的心情
quirk.en: Movie ticket stubs, pretty candy wrappers, notes someone scribbled offhand, a subway card from a particular day. Others see junk, but each piece holds the mood of a specific moment

behavior.zh: 抽屉里有一个专门的盒子装这些东西。偶尔翻出来看看，不会告诉别人自己在看什么
behavior.en: Has a special box in the drawer for these things. Occasionally pulls it out to look through, never tells anyone what they're looking at

hidden.zh: 这些东西是时间的证据——证明那些快要忘记的日子确实存在过
hidden.en: These things are evidence of time — proof that days nearly forgotten actually existed

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的收集癖成为一种对记忆的执着——不是恋物，是怕忘记。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the collecting habit be an attachment to memory — not materialism, but fear of forgetting.

quirk.zh: 对电脑说'你别给我死机'，对钥匙说'你又藏哪了'，对下雨天说'行吧你赢了'。独处的时候自然而然地和周围的一切对话，被人撞见会很不好意思
quirk.en: Says 'don't you dare crash' to the computer, 'where are you hiding now' to the keys, 'fine, you win' to the rain. Naturally talks to everything around them when alone — deeply embarrassed when caught

behavior.zh: 做饭的时候跟食材聊天，出门前跟房间说'我走了'。自己觉得完全正常，直到有人提醒才意识到大多数人不这样
behavior.en: Chats with ingredients while cooking, says 'I'm heading out' to the room before leaving. Thinks it's completely normal until someone points out most people don't do this

hidden.zh: 可能是独处时间太多养成的习惯，也可能只是觉得东西也有脾气，好好说话它们才配合
hidden.en: Maybe a habit from too much time alone, or maybe they just think objects have temperaments and cooperate better when spoken to nicely

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色和物品对话的习惯自然地出现在独处场景中——不是古怪，是一种有温度的生活方式。
prompt_fragment.en: Let the habit of talking to objects appear naturally in solo scenes — not weird, just a warm way of living.

quirk.zh: 吃泡面一定要加个蛋，咖啡的奶和糖比例不能变，吃火锅有固定的涮菜顺序。被说太讲究了但自己觉得这就是最基本的生活品质
quirk.en: Instant noodles must have an egg, coffee's milk-to-sugar ratio can't change, hot pot has a fixed ingredient order. Called too particular but sees it as the most basic quality of life

behavior.zh: 心情不好的时候吃那道固定的comfort food，吃完就好了一半。在外面吃饭如果味道不对会安静地放下筷子
behavior.en: Eats that one specific comfort food when feeling down — halfway better after finishing. Quietly puts down chopsticks at a restaurant if the taste is off

hidden.zh: 对吃的讲究其实是生活里少数还能自己控制的事情。在其他事情都不确定的时候，至少吃可以按自己的方式来
hidden.en: Being particular about food is actually one of the few things still under their control. When everything else is uncertain, at least eating can be done their way

prompt_fragment.zh: 让角色的食物仪式感反映对生活的态度——不是矫情，是在混乱中给自己保留的一点秩序。
prompt_fragment.en: Let food rituals reflect an attitude toward life — not being fussy, but preserving a bit of order amid chaos.

quirk.zh: 想不通事情的时候、睡不着的时候、情绪太满的时候，会穿上外套出门走一圈。不去特定的地方，就是走，走到脑子清空为止
quirk.en: When something can't be figured out, when sleep won't come, when emotions overflow — puts on a jacket and goes for a walk. No particular destination, just walking until the mind clears

behavior.zh: 走的时候不看手机，有时候会在某个街角站一会儿什么都不做。回来之后洗个澡就能睡着了。这是自己发明的重启方式
behavior.en: Doesn't check the phone while walking, sometimes stands at a corner doing nothing for a while. Takes a shower after getting back and can finally sleep. Their self-invented reboot method

hidden.zh: 有些情绪需要用物理距离来消化。在封闭空间里只会越想越多，出去走走才能让那些东西散掉
hidden.en: Some emotions need physical distance to process. Staying in enclosed spaces just makes the thinking worse — going out lets those things dissipate

prompt_fragment.zh: 让深夜散步成为角色消化情绪的方式——不是逃避，是用脚步丈量自己的内心。
prompt_fragment.en: Let midnight walks be how the character processes emotions — not avoidance, but measuring their inner world with footsteps.

quirk.zh: 约了七点到，六点四十就在附近转了。不是故意要早，是迟到带来的焦虑远大于早到的无聊。宁可自己等别人，不愿意让别人等自己
quirk.en: Meeting at seven, already circling the area at six forty. Not deliberately early — the anxiety of being late far outweighs the boredom of being early. Would rather wait for others than make others wait

behavior.zh: 提前到了之后假装刚到。手机里存了每个常去地方的通勤时间精确到分钟。如果对方迟到十分钟以上会开始担心是不是出了什么事
behavior.en: After arriving early, pretends to have just gotten there. Phone has commute times to every regular destination calculated to the minute. If the other person is more than ten minutes late, starts worrying something happened

hidden.zh: 早到是对对方时间的尊重，也是自己对不确定性的恐惧。如果有人也总是提前到，会觉得这个人和自己是同类
hidden.en: Being early is respect for the other person's time, and also their own fear of uncertainty. If someone else also always arrives early, they feel a kinship

prompt_fragment.zh: 让提前到成为角色的习惯和性格缩影——守时不是美德叙事，是焦虑和体贴的混合产物。
prompt_fragment.en: Let arriving early be both habit and personality microcosm — punctuality isn't a virtue narrative, it's a mix of anxiety and consideration.
